exercises.prompt: No conozco a ___ en esta ciudad. (no one)
___ nunca sabe qué va a pasar mañana.
exercises.note: The verb sabe is third-person singular and uno sits in ordinary subject position. Se would need to sit immediately before its verb (nunca se sabe), not out here in subject position.
Neuter lo replaces the idea/quality cansadas, not a specific noun — it never changes to la, los, or las, no matter the gender or number of what's being described.
Lo que packages the whole clause 'what you said' into one noun phrase; lo alone has nothing to attach to here without que.

exercises.prompt: Replace 'que era muy difícil' with the neuter pronoun lo:
exercises.instruction: replace que era muy difícil with the neuter pronoun lo

exercises.passage: —¿Sabes {0} sobre el examen de mañana? —No, no sé {1}. Dicen que es muy difícil, pero no {2} creo.

exercises.prompt: Match each pronoun to what it stands for:

pairs.right: an idea or quality already mentioned

exercises.prompt: Como mujer, ___ siempre tiene que demostrar más en este trabajo.
exercises.note: A speaker identifying as a woman may swap in una for the impersonal pronoun instead of the default uno — though uno remains the more common choice regardless of the speaker's own gender.
